what: Maps the game's accuracy_circle_scale (characters.json) to on-screen reticle diameter in pixels, at the fixed scope-lock recording resolution (2622x1206).

relation.notes: Linear with offset, NOT proportional. Slope ~3/4 px per scale-unit; ~34-scale dead zone (below scale 34 the circle renders as ~0px). Fit from 3 independent points; residuals <=2.6px.

crossCheck.finding: AR 75-scale circle (29px) == Noir mid-band boss core (28px), measured independently. Mid is the AR's optimal range.
crossCheck.meaning: At optimal range the AR accuracy circle shrinks to the boss-core diameter => every shot inside the accuracy zone is a core hit. This is the design definition of optimal range, and it independently validates the scale->pixel map (the core never entered the regression yet lands on the predicted AR circle).

coreDiameterVsRange.selection: Inverse-with-offset (perspective projection: apparent size ~ 1/(distance + camera offset)). Pure inverse d=k/r is RULED OUT (near d*r maxes 775 < far d*r mins 952, no overlap within range bounds). Linear d=a-b*r RULED OUT (mid-far implied range 57.7 exceeds its 35-55 bound). Inverse-offset is the only tested form with all four bands in-bounds.
coreDiameterVsRange.notes: k, c ride on fuzzy range midpoints (treat +/-10%); the FORM is robustly selected by the bounds. One hard range value at any band would pin k,c exactly.

approximationCaveat: ALL numbers here (both relations, all measured diameters, hit/miss areas) are APPROXIMATIONS that are logically self-consistent and cross-validated (e.g. AR optimal-circle == mid core to 1px), intended as a principled BASELINE. They cannot reach true in-game accuracy without the game source code (exact reticle render formula, camera params, bloom RNG). Use as a grounded starting point, refine with more measurements, never treat as measured-truth.
usage: Chain: (1) accuracy_circle_scale -> circle px via 0.751*scale-25; (2) core px -> range via 2100/coreD-47 (or range -> core px). Together they give circle-vs-core geometry at any range for AR/SMG/SG, feeding core-hit-rate and SG pellet-landing. Worked SG example: noir-sg-bands.json.
